# Touchstone QA

This repo uses **Touchstone**, a Playwright-based QA framework. **Running QA needs no AI** — it's plain
Node + Playwright: `npm run qa` (ship-confidence verdict) and `touchstone <doctor|serve|perf|audit|mutation|summary|knowledge|auth-save>`.

## QA skills (agent-agnostic procedures)
When a task matches one of these, READ the referenced file and follow its steps. Where a step names a
Claude-specific tool (a preview/screenshot MCP, `spawn_task`), use your agent's equivalent (browser
automation, sub-tasks); the deterministic parts (bash, file edits, `npm run qa`) work in any agent.
All skills are READ-ONLY on app source — they write tests/cards/config, never app code.

- **qa-audit** — Scan project dependencies for known CVEs (supply-chain audit). Polyglot — detects the ecosystem and runs osv-scanner or npm audit. Use when the user asks to check vulnerabilities, CVEs, dependency security, npm audit, or supply chain.
  → read `../../../.npm-global/lib/node_modules/touchstone/skills/qa-audit/SKILL.md` and follow it.
- **qa-author** — Author a FULL, multi-category test suite for a feature using the .qa framework. When the user builds/changes a feature and wants comprehensive coverage ("write tests for X", "/qa-author X", "cover this feature"), this scaffolds a feature folder with specs across the relevant quality categories (functional, db, security, api, e2e, ux, visual, a11y, perf, mcp), wires them to the framework, runs `npm run qa`, and iterates to a green comprehensive report.
  → read `../../../.npm-global/lib/node_modules/touchstone/skills/qa-author/SKILL.md` and follow it.
- **qa-bughunter** — AI exploratory bug-hunting — drive the running app (in the safe test env) like a curious QA tester to find bugs scripted tests miss: broken layouts, console errors, dead controls, bad error states, UX problems, permission leaks. Read-only on source; reports prioritized findings with repro + evidence. Opt-in/manual (spends LLM tokens), never per-commit. It also CODIFIES confirmed flows + bugs into scripted regression tests so the next run is automated (no tokens) and exploration moves to new ground. Use for "hunt for bugs / exploratory QA / try to break <feature> / /qa-bughunter".
  → read `../../../.npm-global/lib/node_modules/touchstone/skills/qa-bughunter/SKILL.md` and follow it.
- **qa-discover** — Scan a repository to discover EVERY external network resource it depends on (databases, object storage, mail/SMS/push, geo/maps, weather, OAuth, payments, analytics, AI APIs, file-import like Google Drive/Photos, webhooks, caches, queues…), classify each, recommend a QA strategy (test-env / mock / capture / bypass / sandbox / skip), and write a resource manifest (.qa/app-qa/qa-resources.mjs). This is the "prepare QA" step — run it when adopting the framework or whenever dependencies change. Use for "what does this app talk to / what do we need to set up for QA / discover external services / /qa-discover".
  → read `../../../.npm-global/lib/node_modules/touchstone/skills/qa-discover/SKILL.md` and follow it.
- **qa-extend** — Author a Touchstone extension adapter so an app plugs its OWN backend or tool into a framework step WITHOUT forking the framework — a non-Mongo database lifecycle, a code scanner (Sonar/Snyk/CodeQL/ Checkmarx/Trivy → the scan:<name> signal), a coverage tool/ecosystem (JaCoCo/SimpleCov/lcov/cobertura → the coverage signal), a launcher/backing rig (docker/MQ/device farms, including rig-owned evidence), a findings tracker (Jira/Bitbucket/webhook), or an auth strategy. Knows the contract Touchstone expects at each seam, scaffolds the gated placeholder, implements it for the specific tool, and makes the conformance self-test green. Use for "plug in / integrate / ingest <tool> into touchstone", "add Sonar/Snyk/JaCoCo/Couchbase to the verdict", "extend touchstone / write an adapter / /qa-extend".
  → read `../../../.npm-global/lib/node_modules/touchstone/skills/qa-extend/SKILL.md` and follow it.
- **qa-framework-health** — Diagnose where TOUCHSTONE ITSELF is struggling — for the framework developer, not the app team. Reads the friction journal(s) (`touchstone journal --json`: guard refusals, preflight failures, adapter-gate rejections, crashed collections, gatherer errors, KB recall misses, MCP tool errors) plus the skill-eval score history, clusters recurring pain, ranks it, and proposes concrete framework fixes with file:line. Use for "how is the framework doing / where does touchstone struggle / framework health / what should I improve next / /qa-framework-health".
  → read `../../../.npm-global/lib/node_modules/touchstone/skills/qa-framework-health/SKILL.md` and follow it.
- **qa-learn** — Distill the reusable knowledge from a QA run / exploratory session / fixed gap into Knowledge Base "playbook cards" so future projects start smarter. Run after onboarding a new app/target, after a qa-bughunter run, after fixing a framework gap/footgun, or at the end of an early-project run. Use for "capture what we learned / write a knowledge card / record this technique / qa-learn".
  → read `../../../.npm-global/lib/node_modules/touchstone/skills/qa-learn/SKILL.md` and follow it.
- **qa-mutation** — Run mutation testing (Stryker) to prove the test suite actually CATCHES bugs, not just passes — it mutates app source (flips operators, drops statements) and reports the % of mutants the tests kill. Surviving mutants = behavior your tests don't assert. Use for "/qa-mutation", "mutation testing", "are the tests actually good", "do the tests catch bugs", "mutation score". Scoped + opt-in (slow). READ-ONLY on app source — propose tests that kill survivors, never edit app code.
  → read `../../../.npm-global/lib/node_modules/touchstone/skills/qa-mutation/SKILL.md` and follow it.
- **qa-perf** — Run Lighthouse performance / accessibility / best-practices / SEO budgets against the safe test app and report misses. Use when the user asks to check performance, Lighthouse, web vitals, page speed, or quality budgets.
  → read `../../../.npm-global/lib/node_modules/touchstone/skills/qa-perf/SKILL.md` and follow it.
- **qa-project-setup** — Orchestrate the full QA onboarding for an app, end-to-end, in your agent: discover its external resources (qa-discover) → prepare the test seams (env/test-DB/auth/mocks) → author real test suites across categories (qa-author) → run the deterministic self-test (npm run qa) and iterate to a green/honest verdict. This is the one guided flow after `touchstone init` has scaffolded `.qa/`. It SEQUENCES the other skills and adds the setup gates between them — it does not replace them. Stops at the green self-test; exploratory bug-hunting (qa-bughunter) is the optional next step. READ-ONLY on app source (writes tests/.qa/config, never app code). Runs on YOUR agent's model — use your strongest, it reasons over the whole repo and spends tokens (opt-in). Use for "set up QA for this project end-to-end / onboard this app / /qa-project-setup".
  → read `../../../.npm-global/lib/node_modules/touchstone/skills/qa-project-setup/SKILL.md` and follow it.
- **qa-report** — Produce a clean, shareable summary report of the latest Touchstone QA run — verdict, coverage, per-category scores, failures with root-cause from the captured evidence, device gallery, host metrics, and trend — plus prioritized next actions. Runs the deterministic generator then layers an AI narrative on top. READ-ONLY on app source (propose fixes with file:line, never edit). Use for "/qa-report", "summarize the last run", "generate a run report", "what happened in the QA run", "is it shippable".
  → read `../../../.npm-global/lib/node_modules/touchstone/skills/qa-report/SKILL.md` and follow it.
- **qa-scaffold** — Scaffold a BASELINE UI test suite across a whole app by crawling it. Runs `touchstone crawl` to enumerate every surface (deduped: /trips/123 + /trips/456 → one /trips/:id), then authors one meaningful baseline spec per surface using qa-author's conventions — a starter suite the testing team extends. Use for "scaffold tests for the whole app", "give the team a starting test suite", "/qa-scaffold". NOT bug-hunting (that's qa-bughunter) and NOT deep single-feature coverage (that's qa-author) — this is broad, shallow, per-surface.
  → read `../../../.npm-global/lib/node_modules/touchstone/skills/qa-scaffold/SKILL.md` and follow it.
- **qa-security-review** — AI security review of the app's OWN source code (SAST + secret scanning). Runs semgrep (code issues) and gitleaks (hard-coded credentials) over app.sourceDir, then triages the findings (kills false positives) and adds a business-logic pass the scanners can't do — injection, authz/IDOR, secrets, SSRF, unsafe deserialization, weak crypto, path traversal. Reports real findings with file:line + a fix, folds `sast` and `secrets` signals into the ship verdict. Use for "security review the source / SAST / find vulnerabilities in the code / find hard-coded secrets / gitleaks / review app.sourceDir for security / /qa-security-review". READ-ONLY on app source.
  → read `../../../.npm-global/lib/node_modules/touchstone/skills/qa-security-review/SKILL.md` and follow it.
- **qa-setup** — Generate a clean, runnable per-resource QA setup skill into .qa/app-qa/skills/ for an external resource — especially a 🟡 todo from `npm run qa:doctor`. It reads the resource's strategy (mock / test-env / capture / sandbox) from the manifest + service playbook and writes a concrete, copy-pasteable skill the user runs (in whatever environment they choose) to make the resource QA-ready. Use for "set up <service> for QA / create a mock for <service> / prepare a todo resource / /qa-setup <name>".
  → read `../../../.npm-global/lib/node_modules/touchstone/skills/qa-setup/SKILL.md` and follow it.
- **qa-setup-ci** — Set up Continuous Integration for the QA framework as part of QA setup — cost-consciously. Lets the user pick WHERE (local git pre-push hook = zero cloud cost, or GitHub Actions, or both) and WHEN (never on every commit by default: PR-to-main + manual, optional nightly). Generates the chosen config (a pre-push hook and/or .github/workflows/qa.yml) wired for ephemeral Mongo + local storage + Playwright browsers, and uploads the comprehensive report. Use for "set up CI / run QA in CI / /qa-setup-ci / automate qa".
  → read `../../../.npm-global/lib/node_modules/touchstone/skills/qa-setup-ci/SKILL.md` and follow it.
- **visual-qa** — AI exploratory VISUAL bug-hunting — drive the running app (in the safe test env) and JUDGE the rendered pixels across themes × viewports × states to catch what deterministic pixel-diffing can't: dark-mode white-on-white, contrast failures, layout overflow, clipped/overlapping elements, responsive breakage, broken images, bad empty/error states. Read-only on source; reports prioritized findings with the screenshot as evidence. It also CODIFIES confirmed-good views into deterministic @visual baselines so the next run regresses for free (no tokens, no vision). Opt-in (spends vision tokens), never per-commit. Use for "visual QA / does the UI look right / review the design / check dark mode / responsive / /visual-qa".
  → read `../../../.npm-global/lib/node_modules/touchstone/skills/visual-qa/SKILL.md` and follow it.

## Learned playbook (Knowledge Base)
Before hand-rolling, recall proven recipes/techniques/gotchas: `touchstone knowledge <list | search <text> | show <id>>`.

## Browser tools (for the browser-driving skills: qa-bughunter, visual-qa)
These skills need a browser. Use your agent's tool for each capability:

**Codex / Cursor / Gemini / any MCP agent → Playwright MCP (`npx @playwright/mcp@latest`, add to your MCP config)**

| capability | what | tool |
|---|---|---|
| open | boot the safe test app + a browser (or point at a URL) | `touchstone serve + browser_install` |
| navigate | go to a route / reload / back | `browser_navigate` |
| screenshot | capture the rendered page | `browser_take_screenshot` |
| snapshot | read the DOM / accessibility tree (text) | `browser_snapshot` |
| console | read console messages (errors) | `browser_console_messages` |
| network | read requests (failed / 4xx / 5xx) | `browser_network_requests` |
| click | click an element | `browser_click` |
| fill | type into a field | `browser_type` |
| resize | set the viewport (mobile / tablet / desktop) | `browser_resize` |
| eval | run JS in the page (debug only) | `browser_evaluate` |
| close | stop the browser / app | `browser_close` |

**No browser MCP? Terminal fallback — zero setup, Playwright is already a dependency:**
1. `touchstone serve` boots the app in the safe test env (isolated DB, mocked externals, local login).
2. Drive it with a short `@playwright/test` script: `page.goto()`, `page.screenshot()`,
   `page.locator(sel).click()`/`.fill()`, and read `page.on('console')` / `page.on('response')` for the
   console/network evidence. Same capabilities, via the terminal every agent has.

<!-- touchstone:end -->
